SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.compressor

Class AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.compressor.AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem extends Object implements Serializable
Screening-level advanced anti-surge control utilities for centrifugal compressor studies.

This class collects the public, non-vendor-certified anti-surge methods that are commonly described in open literature: reduced-coordinate surge comparison, transmitter validation and voting, recycle-valve/piping-volume screening, dual hot/cold recycle valve command splitting, simple fault-tolerant override logic, heuristic fuzzy and predictive/MPC-style valve demand, and a lumped educational surge-oscillation model.

The calculations are intended for simulation, teaching, screening, and controller-logic regression tests. They are not a replacement for compressor vendor anti-surge controllers, site safety instrumented functions, certified machine protection systems, or project-specific sizing calculations.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem

      public AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem()
      Default constructor.

  • Method Details

    • calculateReducedCoordinates

      public static AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.ReducedCoordinatePoint calculateReducedCoordinates(double suctionPressure, double dischargePressure, double suctionTemperature, double dischargeTemperature, double flowMeterDeltaPressure)
      Calculate reduced head and reduced-flow coordinates from process measurements.

      The reduced head follows the common anti-surge literature form ((Pd / Ps)^sigma - 1) / sigma. The reduced flow follows the pressure-normalized flowmeter signal dPflow / Ps. These coordinates are useful for composition-robust surge-line representation when the same units are used consistently for pressure and flowmeter differential pressure.

      Parameters:
      suctionPressure - suction pressure in any absolute pressure unit, must be positive
      dischargePressure - discharge pressure in the same absolute pressure unit, must be positive
      suctionTemperature - suction temperature in Kelvin, must be positive
      dischargeTemperature - discharge temperature in Kelvin, must be positive
      flowMeterDeltaPressure - flowmeter differential pressure in the same pressure unit as suction pressure
      Returns:
      reduced coordinate point
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if an input is non-finite or physically invalid

    • calculateInvariantMapPoint

      public static AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.InvariantMapPoint calculateInvariantMapPoint(double flow, double head, double speed)
      Convert compressor head and flow to fan-law invariant coordinates.
      Parameters:
      flow - actual inlet flow in m3/hr or any consistent volumetric unit
      head - compressor head in kJ/kg or any consistent head unit
      speed - compressor speed in rpm, must be positive
      Returns:
      invariant map point where reduced flow is flow / speed and reduced head is head / speed^2
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if speed is non-positive or any value is non-finite

    • distanceToReducedControlLine

      public static double distanceToReducedControlLine(double reducedFlow, double surgeReducedFlow, double controlMargin)
      Calculate distance from a reduced-flow point to a reduced surge-control line.
      Parameters:
      reducedFlow - current reduced flow
      surgeReducedFlow - reduced flow at the physical surge line
      controlMargin - fractional control margin above the surge line, e.g. 0.10 for 10%
      Returns:
      dimensionless distance to the surge-control line, positive on the safe side

    • calculateValveCommand

      public double calculateValveCommand(double measuredMargin, double marginRate, double dt)
      Calculate a recycle valve command from measured margin and margin approach rate.
      Parameters:
      measuredMargin - measured distance to surge
      marginRate - rate of change of margin in margin units per second
      dt - timestep in seconds
      Returns:
      valve command in percent opening

    • splitDualRecycleCommand

      public AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.DualRecycleValveCommand splitDualRecycleCommand(double totalValveDemand, double measuredMargin, double marginRate)
      Split total anti-surge demand between normal cold recycle and fast hot recycle valves.
      Parameters:
      totalValveDemand - total valve opening demand in percent
      measuredMargin - measured distance to surge
      marginRate - rate of change of margin in margin units per second
      Returns:
      hot/cold valve command pair

    • evaluateFaultTolerant

      public AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.FaultTolerantDecision evaluateFaultTolerant(List<AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.InstrumentSignal> signals, AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.VotingMode mode, double marginRate, double dt)
      Evaluate redundant anti-surge transmitter signals and calculate a fault-tolerant valve command.
      Parameters:
      signals - transmitter signals to validate and vote
      mode - voting mode
      marginRate - voted margin rate in margin units per second
      dt - timestep in seconds
      Returns:
      fault-tolerant controller decision

    • vote

      public static AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.VotingResult vote(List<AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.InstrumentSignal> signals, AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.VotingMode mode, double maxDeviation)
      Vote redundant transmitter readings after validation.
      Parameters:
      signals - transmitter signals
      mode - voting mode
      maxDeviation - maximum accepted spread before degraded status is reported
      Returns:
      voting result

    • sizeRecycleSystem

      public static AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.RecycleSizingResult sizeRecycleSystem(double inletFlow, double surgeFlow, double controlMargin, double suctionDensity, double valvePressureDrop, double pipingVolume, double requiredResponseTime)
      Size recycle flow and screen valve/piping volume response.
      Parameters:
      inletFlow - current compressor inlet volumetric flow in m3/hr
      surgeFlow - compressor surge volumetric flow in m3/hr
      controlMargin - fractional control margin above surge flow
      suctionDensity - suction gas density in kg/m3
      valvePressureDrop - pressure drop available across recycle valve in bar
      pipingVolume - suction/recycle volume to be filled in m3
      requiredResponseTime - required response time in seconds
      Returns:
      recycle sizing result

    • stepSurgeOscillation

      public static AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.SurgeOscillationState stepSurgeOscillation(AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.SurgeOscillationState state, double compressorPressureRise, double throttleDemand, double recycleFlow, double dt)
      Step a lumped educational surge-oscillation model.

      This is a small Greitzer-inspired two-state oscillator for controller screening. It is intentionally not a rigorous one-dimensional compressor/piping solver.

      Parameters:
      state - current model state
      compressorPressureRise - normalized compressor pressure-rise forcing
      throttleDemand - normalized downstream throttle demand
      recycleFlow - normalized recycle flow, increasing flow away from surge
      dt - timestep in seconds
      Returns:
      next state

    • getCertificationStatus

      public AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.CertificationStatus getCertificationStatus()
      Get the certification status for these public screening methods.
      Returns:
      always AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.CertificationStatus.NOT_VENDOR_CERTIFIED

    • setAlgorithm

      public void setAlgorithm(AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.ControlAlgorithm algorithm)
      Set controller algorithm.
      Parameters:
      algorithm - controller algorithm; null selects predictive PI

    • getAlgorithm

      public AdvancedAntiSurgeControlSystem.ControlAlgorithm getAlgorithm()
      Get controller algorithm.
      Returns:
      selected algorithm

    • setMarginSetPoint

      public void setMarginSetPoint(double marginSetPoint)
      Set margin set point.
      Parameters:
      marginSetPoint - dimensionless margin set point

    • setPiTuning

      public void setPiTuning(double proportionalGain, double integralTime)
      Set PI tuning parameters.
      Parameters:
      proportionalGain - proportional gain in valve percent per unit margin error
      integralTime - integral time in seconds; zero disables integral action

    • setPredictionHorizon

      public void setPredictionHorizon(double predictionHorizon)
      Set prediction horizon.
      Parameters:
      predictionHorizon - prediction horizon in seconds

    • setOpeningRange

      public void setOpeningRange(double minOpening, double maxOpening)
      Set valve opening range.
      Parameters:
      minOpening - minimum opening in percent
      maxOpening - maximum opening in percent

    • setMarginGainPerValvePercent

      public void setMarginGainPerValvePercent(double marginGainPerValvePercent)
      Set estimated margin gain per valve percent for screening MPC.
      Parameters:
      marginGainPerValvePercent - margin gain per percent opening

    • setDualValveActivation

      public void setDualValveActivation(double hotValveMargin, double fastApproachRate)
      Set dual-valve activation limits.
      Parameters:
      hotValveMargin - margin below which hot recycle starts opening
      fastApproachRate - negative margin rate that triggers hot recycle opening

    • setVotingDeviationLimit

      public void setVotingDeviationLimit(double votingDeviationLimit)
      Set voting deviation limit.
      Parameters:
      votingDeviationLimit - maximum spread before degraded voting status

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Reset integral state.

      Calculate rule-based fuzzy valve demand.
      Parameters:

      marginRate - margin rate
      Returns:
      valve demand in percent

      Calculate one-step screening MPC valve demand.

      measuredMargin - measured margin
      marginRate - margin rate
      Returns:
      valve demand in percent

      value - value
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if value is not positive and finite

      low - lower limit
      high - upper limit
      Returns:
      clamped value
